channel.title: SevenAtSevenLastSection
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenLastSection, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Creamy Sausage Spaghetti
item.description: If you love a creamy pasta with big flavor, this creamy sausage spaghetti needs to be on your menu. Smoked…

item.title: A Book for Schitt’s Creek Fans, Norse Mythology, and More New YA Releases For March 4, 2026
item.description: Doing Death's dirty work, Norse mythology, a family-centered story for fans of Schitt's Creek, and more of this week's dynamite YA book releases.

item.title: Savoring the Journey with Padma Lakshmi
item.description: The Emmy-nominated TV host dishes on the power of connection, food, fitness, and the art of finding joy.

item.description: This month is full of heavy hitters, including everything from hefty biographies to quiet ruminations on the small beauties in life.

item.title: Sleuth Into March’s New Mystery & Thriller Adaptations
item.description: Watch a hot mess PI in Florida, Nicole Kidman and Jamie Lee Curtis as sisters in a medical thriller, and more in this month's adaptations.

item.title: It’s Dangerous to Feel This Desperate: How to Ease the Chaos in New York’s Prisons
item.description: When the governor doesn’t commute sentences, and the legislature won’t act, the carrot-and-stick system of rehabilitation disintegrates.

item.description: Parents and children from a detention center in Texas found themselves dropped at a border town shelter with few means to leave.

item.title: The Documentary ‘Exodus’ Paints a Nuanced Picture of Life After Lockup
item.description: Shot over two years, the Critics’ Choice-nominated film values quiet moments. “We’re trying to refuse a spectacle,” says director Nimco Sheikhaden.

item.title: Mississippi’s Black Voters Brace for Elections Ruling That Could Gut Supreme Court Clout
item.description: Black Mississippians won a Voting Rights Act case that challenges how the state elects supreme court justices. But that victory may be in jeopardy.
